domain: Hagfish slime gland defense — compact thread skeins (~150μm) release into seawater and unravel 1000x in length, 25,000x in volume on mechanical agitation, choking predator gills.
specific_mechanism: Pre-fabricated compact protein skein stored in gland; on trigger, ejected to environment where seawater + agitation cause rapid unfurling; volume/length expansion creates immobilizing slime manifold.
llm_application: HAGFISH-SKEIN: store a compact rank-r refusal seed (Wseed ∈ ℝ^(r×d)) per layer. On jailbreak classifier trigger, multiply Wseed by a learned expansion operator E ∈ ℝ^(d×r) with adversary-feature-dependent gain, producing a high-rank defensive activation manifold that occupies the next K layers' attention capacity ('clogs' the adversarial signal). Expansion factor is tunable and adversary-dependent.
novelty_claim: LoRA factorizes high-rank into low-rank for efficiency (offline training). Defensive activation steering uses fixed-shape outputs. ASH-LINE pre-consumes capacity with high-entropy tokens. None propose a compact-seed-stored-defense that EXPANDS by a tunable adversary-dependent factor ON DEMAND during inference to occupy activation capacity defensively.
